key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation model with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration classification across fundamentally different welding processes, including TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical multi-process weld monitoring with far less manual relabeling, improving deployment robustness when changing materials, parameters, or heat-source physics.
method: Train a UDA model to learn domain-invariant penetration features.
Use gradual source domain expansion to bridge source-target process gaps.
Validate transfer on same-process and cross-process TIG/laser datasets.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average accuracies of 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG→laser and 81.13% laser→TIG in cross-process transfer, all well above the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the availability of representative unlabeled target data and careful tuning of adaptation to avoid negative transfer across very different welding physics.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
